zh.Walton upon Trent, Derbyshire, England: 英國英格蘭德比夏爾沃爾頓 武朋 特倫特
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Robert Breton of Walton於1290年出生在英國德比夏爾沃爾頓時，他的父親Sir Roger II Brett , Lord of Walton30歲，母親Bamburga Roberta de Deincourt6歲。他於1323年之後與Philippa of Derbyshire在英國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1350年5月14日在他的家鄉享年60歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Robert Breton of Walton于1290年出生在英国德比夏爾沃爾頓时，他的父亲Sir Roger II Brett , Lord of Walton30岁，母亲Bamburga Roberta de Deincourt6岁。他于1323年之后与Philippa of Derbyshire在英国结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1350年5月14日在他的家乡享年60岁。

nameMeanings.name: Breton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Normand, Renald, Adelard, Alphonse, Armand, Gaetan, Laurent, Serge, Adrien, Aime.
French, Breton (mainly Finistère), and English (of Norman origin): ethnic name for a Breton, from Old French breton, oblique case of bret (see Bret ). Compare Lebreton , see also English Brett .

History: In North America, this surname was originally also a secondary surname or dit (‘also called’) name, borne by Jean Élie/Hélie dit (Le) Breton from Ménéac in Morbihan, Brittany (France), who married Jeanne Labbé in Sainte-Famille, Île d'Orléans, QC, in 1669. His descendants also bear the surname Elie .

variants.name: Bret
meanings: French: ethnic name for a Breton, from Old French bret, nominative of breton ‘Breton’ (see Breton ). See also English Brett .

variants.name: Brittain
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Anglo-Norman French Bretagne, Bretaign(e) ‘Brittany’. Some of the modern forms are from the Middle English Brytane, Brittan ‘Breton’; compare Breton .

variants.name: Britton
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of Breton , and ethnic name for a Breton, from Old French Breton, the oblique case form of Old French Bret; compare Brittain and Brett .

variants.name: Bretton
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: variant of   Breton  .

English: habitational name from any of the places called from Bretton in Derbyshire and Yorkshire, both of which mean ‘settlement of the Britons’, from Old English Brettas ‘Briton’ + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Robert Breton of Walton was born in 1290, in Walton, Derbyshire, England, his father, Sir Roger II Brett , Lord of Walton, was 30 and his mother, Bamburga Roberta de Deincourt, was 6. He married Philippa of Derbyshire after 1323, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died on 14 May 1350, in his hometown, at the age of 60.

Он женился на женщине по имени Philippa of Derbyshire после 1323 г. в местности Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер 14 мая 1350 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 60 лет.
